examples.title: D Flip-Flop Implementation
examples.description: Verilog code for edge-triggered D flip-flop with asynchronous reset.
examples.code:
module d_ff (
 input clk, // Clock input
 input rst_n, // Asynchronous active-low reset
 input d, // Data input
 output reg q // Output
);

// Sequential logic block
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
 if (!rst_n) // Reset condition
 q <= 1'b0;
 else // Normal operation
 q <= d; // Sample input at clock edge
end

endmodule
examples.explanation: This D flip-flop samples input D on positive clock edge and holds value until next edge. Asynchronous reset (rst_n) overrides clock for immediate reset. The non-blocking assignment (<=) ensures proper simulation of sequential behavior.

examples.title: JK Flip-Flop Implementation
examples.description: Verilog code for JK flip-flop with synchronous clear.
examples.code:
module jk_ff (
 input clk, // Clock
 input clr, // Synchronous clear
 input j, // J input
 input k, // K input
 output reg q, // Output
 output q_bar // Complement output
);

assign q_bar = ~q; // Continuous assignment for complement

// Characteristic equation: Q+ = J*Q' + K'*Q
always @(posedge clk) begin
 if (clr) // Synchronous clear
 q <= 1'b0;
 else begin
 case ({j, k})
 2'b00: q <= q; // Hold
 2'b01: q <= 1'b0; // Reset
 2'b10: q <= 1'b1; // Set
 2'b11: q <= ~q; // Toggle
 endcase
 end
end

endmodule
examples.explanation: Implements JK flip-flop characteristic equation Q+ = JQ̅ + K̅Q. Case statement handles all four input combinations. Toggle occurs when J=K=1. Synchronous clear has priority over normal operation.

examples.title: 4-bit Shift Register (VHDL)
examples.description: VHDL behavioral model of 4-bit parallel-in serial-out shift register using D flip-flops.
examples.explanation: 4-bit PISO shift register. On load=1, parallel data loaded. On load=0, shifts left each clock. MSB output as serial. Demonstrates sequential logic with state (shift_reg signal) updated on clock edges.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per clock cycle (one shift operation)
